task_desc: Fix wind stimulus setTimeout race condition and remaining frame-rate-dependent idle animations -- (1) rapid air-tool drags cause stale setTimeout callbacks to clear BRAIN.stimulate.wind mid-drag: user drags air tool and releases (main.js:303 schedules setTimeout(2000) to clear wind), then starts a new drag within 2 seconds, the old timeout fires and sets wind=false/windStrength=0 while the user is actively dragging, silently killing the wind stimulus; this is the same class of bug D1.2 fixed for touch timers; replace the setTimeout at main.js:303-306 with a frame-counted timer (like touchResetFrame) or store the timeout ID and clearTimeout it on new drag start, (2) handleCanvasMouseup at main.js:300 unconditionally sets BRAIN.stimulate.wind=true even when the user switched tools away from air mid-drag (D3.1 made isDragging cleanup unconditional but the wind=true assignment should be conditional on whether the original drag was an air drag), (3) three idle animation interpolations in drawing functions were missed by D3.2 and still run per-frame without dt scaling, causing them to converge ~2x faster on 120Hz: antenna twitch lerp at main.js:1052-1053 (multiplier 0.08), leg jitter lerp at main.js:1137 (multiplier 0.05), and wing micro-movement lerp at main.js:1145 (multiplier 0.03); these need dtScale passed from the draw/update path and should use exponential interpolation (Math.pow(factor, dtScale)) matching the pattern established in D3.2. Files: js/main.js.
